name: offload-sidecar
description: MCP tool that offloads file transforms, log triage, and vision tasks so Claude passes file paths, not content — deterministic in-process where possible, else routed across local Ollama tiers (deep/fast, private) or Gemini via the agy CLI (flash/pro, budget-gated so quota exhaustion rejects up front instead of failing mid-call). Formerly ollama-sidecar.

ollama_host.title: Deep tier — Ollama host
ollama_host.description: Base URL of the primary Ollama server: the biggest model you can run, for judgment-heavy low-volume operations (extract_json, redact_secrets, summarize). Typically this machine, e.g. http://localhost:11434.

ollama_model.title: Deep tier — Ollama model
ollama_model.description: Model tag for the deep tier. Must already be pulled on the target Ollama instance. Pick the largest model the host can hold — throughput doesn't matter here.

num_ctx.title: Deep tier — context window (tokens)
num_ctx.description: num_ctx passed to the deep tier, as a numeric string. Larger values allow bigger input files but need more memory.

fast_ollama_host.title: Fast tier — Ollama host (optional)
fast_ollama_host.description: Base URL of a second Ollama server for high-token-throughput bulk operations (clean_text, convert_format) — e.g. a LAN PC whose GPU holds a smaller model entirely in VRAM, like http://your-pc.local:11434. Leave unset to run everything on the deep tier.

fast_ollama_model.title: Fast tier — Ollama model (optional)
fast_ollama_model.description: Model tag for the fast tier — a smaller/faster model that fits fully on the GPU. Leave unset to reuse the deep tier model.

fast_num_ctx.title: Fast tier — context window (tokens, optional)
fast_num_ctx.description: num_ctx for the fast tier, as a numeric string. Leave unset to reuse the deep tier value.

tls_ca_file.title: TLS CA bundle (optional)
tls_ca_file.description: Path to a PEM CA bundle used to verify https Ollama endpoints — for LAN reverse proxies with an mkcert/self-signed CA. Leave unset for standard system verification.

agy_bin.title: Cloud tiers — agy binary (optional)
agy_bin.description: Name or path of the Google Antigravity CLI binary used by the flash/pro tiers. Leave as 'agy' if it's on PATH; the cloud tiers simply error (and local tiers keep working) when it isn't installed. NOTE: cloud tiers send file contents to Google.

agy_flash_model.title: Flash tier — agy model name
agy_flash_model.description: agy display name for the flash tier, exactly as `agy models` prints it. Flash is the cheapest metered bucket on a Gemini subscription — the bulk cloud tier.

agy_pro_model.title: Pro tier — agy model name
agy_pro_model.description: agy display name for the pro tier. Pro burns scarce weekly subscription quota — it is never an operation default and has a deliberately tiny call budget.

agy_flash_per_5h.title: Flash tier — calls per 5h (budget)
agy_flash_per_5h.description: Sliding-window call budget for the flash tier, as a numeric string. When spent, flash calls are REJECTED up front (with a structured error pointing at the local tiers) instead of burning subscription quota. 0 disables the window.

agy_flash_per_week.title: Flash tier — calls per week (budget)
agy_flash_per_week.description: Weekly sliding-window call budget for the flash tier, as a numeric string. Google's weekly cap causes multi-day lockouts when blown — keep this conservative. 0 disables the window.

agy_pro_per_5h.title: Pro tier — calls per 5h (budget)
agy_pro_per_5h.description: Sliding-window call budget for the pro tier, as a numeric string. 0 disables the window.

agy_pro_per_week.title: Pro tier — calls per week (budget)
agy_pro_per_week.description: Weekly sliding-window call budget for the pro tier, as a numeric string. Pro quota on base subscription tiers is scarce and a blown weekly cap can lock the model for up to 7 days. 0 disables the window.
